 {"id":"bd-1f4086c5.1","title":"Integration test: mutation to export latency","description":"Measure time from bd create to JSONL update. Verify <500ms latency. Test with multiple rapid mutations to verify batching.","notes":"Test added to daemon_test.go as TestMutationToExportLatency().

Currently skipped with note that it should be enabled once bd-146 (event-driven daemon) is fully implemented and enabled by default.

The test structure is complete:
1. Sets up test environment with fast debounce (500ms)
2. SingleMutationLatency: measures latency from mutation to JSONL update
3. RapidMutationBatching: verifies multiple mutations batch into single export

Once event-driven mode is default, remove the t.Skip() line and the test will validate <500ms latency.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-10-29T20:49:49.103759-07:00","updated_at":"2025-10-30T17:12:58.195867-07:00","closed_at":"2025-10-29T14:19:19.808139-07:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1f4086c5.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1f4086c5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-10-29T20:49:49.107244-07:00","created_by":"import-remap"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1f64","title":"Add comprehensive tests for config.yaml issue-prefix migration","description":"The GH #209 config.yaml migration lacks test coverage:

Missing tests:
- config.SetIssuePrefix() edge cases (empty file, comments, malformed YAML)
- config.GetIssuePrefix() with various config states
- MigrateConfigToYAML() automatic migration logic
- bd init writing to config.yaml instead of DB
- bd migrate DB→config.yaml migration path

Test scenarios needed:
1. SetIssuePrefix with empty config.yaml
2. SetIssuePrefix with existing config.yaml (preserves other settings)
3. SetIssuePrefix with commented issue-prefix line
4. SetIssuePrefix atomic write (temp file cleanup)
5. GetIssuePrefix fallback behavior
6. MigrateConfigToYAML when config.yaml missing prefix but DB has it
7. MigrateConfigToYAML when both missing (detect from issues)
8. MigrateConfigToYAML when config.yaml already has prefix (no-op)
9. Integration test: fresh bd init writes to config.yaml only
10. Integration test: upgrade from v0.21 DB migrates to config.yaml

Priority 1 because this is a user-facing migration affecting all users upgrading to v0.22.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-11-03T22:33:43.08753-08:00","updated_at":"2025-11-03T22:46:16.306565-08:00","closed_at":"2025-11-03T22:46:16.306565-08:00"}
 {"id":"bd-1fkr","title":"bd-hv01: Storage backend extensibility broken by type assertion","description":"Problem: deletion_tracking.go:69-82 uses type assertion for DeleteIssue which breaks if someone adds a new storage backend.

Fix: Check capability before starting merge or add DeleteIssue to Storage interface.

 {"id":"bd-1h8","title":"Fix compact --analyze/--apply error messages to clarify direct mode requirement","description":"**Problem:**
When users run `bd compact --analyze` with daemon running, they get:
```
Error: compact requires SQLite storage
```

This is misleading because they ARE using SQLite (via daemon), but the command needs DIRECT SQLite access.

**Current behavior:**
- Error message suggests they don't have SQLite
- No hint about using --no-daemon flag
- Related to issue #349 item #1

**Proposed fix:**
1. Update error messages in cmd/bd/compact.go lines 106-114 (analyze) and 121-137 (apply)
2. Add explicit hint: "Use --no-daemon flag to bypass daemon"
3. Change SQLite check error from "requires SQLite storage" to "failed to open database in direct mode"

**Files to modify:**
- cmd/bd/compact.go (lines ~106-137)

**Testing:**
- Run with daemon: `bd compact --analyze` should show clear error + hint
- Run with --no-daemon: `bd compact --analyze --no-daemon` should work
- Verify error message is actionable","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-11-20T20:47:45.606924-05:00","updated_at":"2025-11-20T20:59:13.406952-05:00","closed_at":"2025-11-20T20:59:13.406952-05:00"}

 {"id":"bd-1hu59","title":"Test failures: schema mismatch after owner field addition","description":"Pre-existing test failures detected during refinery patrol:

1. cmd/bd: TestResolveProtoIDOrTitle - sql: expected 31 destination arguments in Scan, not 32
2. internal/storage/sqlite: TestGetIssuesByLabel - same scan mismatch
3. internal/storage/sqlite: TestMigrateContentHashColumn - no such column: owner

Root cause: The 'owner' field was added (ceb5769c) but tests/fixtures are not updated to match the new schema.

Files affected:
- cmd/bd template tests
- internal/storage/sqlite/labels_test.go
- internal/storage/sqlite/migrations_test.go","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-01-10T22:00:41.275253-08:00","created_by":"beads/refinery","updated_at":"2026-01-10T22:00:41.275253-08:00"}
